title: Sometimes containers are suddenly stopped
body:
### Issue Description

The problem is similar to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/11539).

Sometimes containers has status "Created", sometimes "Exited", but i'm pretty sure it is not related to contents of the containers, because i have around 18 containers, some of them completely independent and still they randomly stopping.

My `/usr/lib/tmpfiles.d/podman.conf` file:

```x /tmp/podman-run-*
x /tmp/containers-user-*
x /tmp/run-*/libpod
x /run/user/1000
D! /run/podman 0700 root root
D! /var/lib/cni/networks```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. unknown

### Describe the results you received

containers "randomly" stopping after few hours or something

### Describe the results you expected

24/7 up

_No response_

comments.body:
Note that v3.4 is very old, I would recommend you try with the latest upstream release first.

Stopping randomly is not something we can work with, we need exact reproducers. Also keep in mind that a containers exits when the main process exits, so are you sure that it is not you container process that is exiting? Did you check the container logs?

But i can't see newer version in Ubuntu repos.
Is says `3.4.4+ds1-1ubuntu1 amd64` is the latest...

![2023-02-16 10-35-24 Screenshot](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/38214332/219298523-edfddca9-fb40-4c6b-a7b5-e1490998817d.png)

Okay i checked every container log again and what i can say: they all were shut down properly.

examples:

mongo (exited 0)

```
{"t":{"$date":"2023-02-15T15:58:23.961+00:00"},"s":"I",  "c":"CONTROL",  "id":23377,   "ctx":"SignalHandler","msg":"Received signal","attr":{"signal":15,"error":"Terminated"}}
{"t":{"$date":"2023-02-15T15:58:23.962+00:00"},"s":"I",  "c":"CONTROL",  "id":23378,   "ctx":"SignalHandler","msg":"Signal was sent by kill(2)","attr":{"pid":0,"uid":0}}
{"t":{"$date":"2023-02-15T15:58:23.962+00:00"},"s":"I",  "c":"CONTROL",  "id":23381,   "ctx":"SignalHandler","msg":"will terminate after current cmd ends"}
{"t":{"$date":"2023-02-15T15:58:23.962+00:00"},"s":"I",  "c":"REPL",     "id":4784900, "ctx":"SignalHandler","msg":"Stepping down the ReplicationCoordinator for shutdown","attr":{"waitTimeMillis":10000}}
```

frontend application (exited 0)

```info: Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime[14]
      Now listening on: http://0.0.0.0:5000
info: Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime[0]
      Application started. Press Ctrl+C to shut down.
info: Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime[0]
      Hosting environment: Production
info: Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime[0]
      Content root path: /usr/app
info: Microsoft.Hosting.Lifetime[0]
      Application is shutting down...
```

postgresql (created)

```2023-02-15 14:15:43.384 UTC [1] LOG:  database system is ready to accept connections
2023-02-15 14:20:43.438 UTC [22] LOG:  checkpoint starting: time
2023-02-15 14:21:06.762 UTC [22] LOG:  checkpoint complete: wrote 230 buffers (0.2%); 0 WAL file(s) added, 0 removed, 1 recycled; write=22.918 s, sync=0.319 s, total=23.325 s; sync files=52, longest=0.031 s, average=0.007 s; distance=1383 kB, estimate=1383 kB
2023-02-15 15:58:23.964 UTC [1] LOG:  received smart shutdown request
2023-02-15 15:58:23.988 UTC [1] LOG:  background worker "logical replication launcher" (PID 27) exited with exit code 1
2023-02-15 15:58:23.988 UTC [22] LOG:  shutting down
2023-02-15 15:58:24.023 UTC [22] LOG:  checkpoint starting: shutdown immediate
2023-02-15 15:58:24.303 UTC [22] LOG:  checkpoint complete: wrote 0 buffers (0.0%); 0 WAL file(s) added, 0 removed, 0 recycled; write=0.076 s, sync=0.001 s, total=0.316 s; sync files=0, longest=0.000 s, average=0.000 s; distance=0 kB, estimate=1245 kB
2023-02-15 15:58:24.406 UTC [1] LOG:  database system is shut down
```

of course no one manually stopping them.
I would think this is something like systemd doing the shutdown.  I don't see how Podman could suddenly start shutting down random containers.

> 

what can be reason for systemd to send kill(2) command (SIGTERM)? 

I can see that is just deactivated suddenly. Sorry i'm not an expert in linux and i'm not even the one who did setup the host, so it possible some basic setting are wrong?

journalctl -u podman.service
```
Feb 15 15:58:05 myhost systemd[1]: podman.service: Deactivated successfully.
Feb 15 15:58:05 myhost systemd[1]: podman.service: Consumed 32.673s CPU time.
```

Okay, the mystery is solved. I don't know right terms to describe it, but it something like this:

my podman service working in user@1000.service, and some people connecting to the host via ssh with the same user. So, when everyone closing connection to the host, systemd killing user@1000.service and podman with it...

Podman containers are removed when user logs out, systemd kills all user processes.

If you enable linger, that should fix the problem

sudo loginctl enable-linger USERNAME
